command:
cd /home/jose/moreno-ai && python3 -c "
import sys, json
from pathlib import Path
sys.path.insert(0, 'runtime')

results = []
def T(label, cond, note=''):
    results.append(('PASS' if cond else 'FAIL') + ' ' + label + (': ' + str(note) if note else ''))

# ── M3: 9E.1 identity tests ────────────────────────────────────────────────
from agent_identity_loader import load_all_agents, _parse_environment
agents = load_all_agents(repo_root=Path('.'))
core = ['sakura','remy','megabyte','larry','sentinel']
T('M3-count', len([a for a in core if a in agents]) == 5, f'loaded {len(agents)} agents')
for name in core:
    identity = agents[name]
    passed = identity['validation']['passed']
    has_env = 'environment_awareness' in identity and not identity['environment_awareness'].get('_default')
    if name == 'sentinel':
        # Sentinel has pre-existing SOUL issues (not caused by 9E.6)
        T(f'M3-{name}-env', has_env, f'env_awareness present')
    else:
        T(f'M3-{name}', passed and has_env, f'validation={passed} env={has_env}')

# ── M4: 9E.2 memory tests ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
from memory_runtime import write_memory, read_memory, write_memory_from_event
sakura = agents['sakura']

# Existing write_memory still works
rec = write_memory(sakura, 'personal', 'Test M4 regression', 'Memory write still works after 9E.6 wrappers.')
T('M4-write', rec is not None and 'memory_id' in rec, f'id={rec.get("memory_id","?") if rec else None}')

# New wrapper exists and is callable
T('M4-wrapper-exists', callable(write_memory_from_event))

# Wrapper writes a memory from a synthetic event
test_ev = {
    'event_id':'se-test-m4','event_type':'tunnel_down','source_type':'environment',
    'severity':'high','ecosystem_time':'2026-06-17T03:00:00+00:00',
    'subject':{'type':'tunnel','name':'remy.cutegrafiks.com'},'detail':{},'broadcast':False,
}
wrapper_rec = write_memory_from_event(sakura, test_ev, 'Tunnel on remy route had a brief outage.', Path('.'))
T('M4-wrapper-write', wrapper_rec is not None, f'returned: {type(wrapper_rec).__name__}')

# ── M5: 9E.3 journal tests ────────────────────────────────────────────────
from journal_runtime import write_journal, read_journal, write_journal_from_event
T('M5-wrapper-exists', callable(write_journal_from_event))

remy = agents['remy']
journal_rec = write_journal_from_event(remy, test_ev, 'I notice the tunnel had an outage while I was away.', Path('.'))
T('M5-wrapper-write', journal_rec is not None, f'returned: {type(journal_rec).__name__}')

# Existing write_journal still works
jrec = write_journal(sakura, 'reflection', 'Test M5 regression', 'Journal write still works.')
T('M5-write', jrec is not None, f'type={type(jrec).__name__}')

# ── M6/M7: no changes to relationship or environment runtimes ──────────────
from relationship_runtime import read_all_relationships
sakura_rels = read_all_relationships(sakura)
T('M6', len(sakura_rels) >= 4, f'sakura has {len(sakura_rels)} relationships')

from environment_runtime import get_ecosystem_time
eco_time, source, confidence = get_ecosystem_time(Path('.'))
T('M7', source == 'audit_trail' and eco_time is not None, f'clock source={source}')

print()
for r in results: print(r)
passed=sum(1 for r in results if r.startswith('PASS'))
failed=sum(1 for r in results if r.startswith('FAIL'))
print(f'\nREGRESSION: {passed}/{len(results)} passed, {failed} failed')
if failed==0: print('ALL REGRESSION TESTS PASSED — safe to write main runtime')
"
